_note: Per-lesson role-play scenario. Grounded in A2/007 dialogue + vocab. Learner plays Mia; computer plays Sam. Each NPC node offers 2-3 branches so the same lesson language can be reached different ways. Consumed by RP.Engine + RP.classifiers.local (see roleplay/).
title: 007 · Saving Money

routes.meaning: Mia asks why Sam is saving money.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Why are you saving money?”

routes.meaning: Mia guesses what Sam is saving for.
examples: Are you saving for a toy?
Do you want a new game?
routes.hint: You can guess, or ask “Why are you saving money?”

partial.message: Ask Sam his reason: “Why are you saving money?”

off_topic.message: Sam is saving money. Ask him “Why are you saving money?”

npc.text: Good guess! But it is not a toy. Ask me why.

routes.meaning: Mia asks why Sam is saving.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Why are you saving money?”

partial.message: Ask him why: “Why are you saving money?”

off_topic.message: Ask Sam “Why are you saving money?”

npc.text: Because I want to buy a new bike.

routes.meaning: Mia asks how much the bike costs.
examples: How much does the bike cost?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “How much does the bike cost?”

routes.meaning: Mia reacts happily to the bike.
routes.hint: You can react, or ask “How much does the bike cost?”

partial.message: Ask about the price: “How much does the bike cost?”

off_topic.message: Sam wants a bike. Ask “How much does the bike cost?”

npc.text: Yes! It is a red bike. I really want it.

routes.meaning: Mia asks how much the bike costs.
examples: How much does the bike cost?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “How much does the bike cost?”

partial.message: Ask the price: “How much does the bike cost?”

off_topic.message: Ask Sam “How much does the bike cost?”

routes.meaning: Mia asks how much money Sam has now.
examples: How much do you have now?
How much money do you have?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “How much do you have now?”

routes.meaning: Mia says fifty dollars is a lot.
examples: Fifty dollars is a lot of money.
routes.hint: You can react, or ask “How much do you have now?”

partial.message: Ask how much he saved: “How much do you have now?”

off_topic.message: The bike is fifty dollars. Ask “How much do you have now?”

npc.text: Yes, it is a lot. That is why I save every week.

routes.meaning: Mia asks how much money Sam has now.
examples: How much do you have now?
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “How much do you have now?”

partial.message: Ask him: “How much do you have now?”

off_topic.message: Ask Sam “How much do you have now?”

routes.meaning: Mia works out that Sam needs twenty more dollars.
examples: So you need twenty more dollars.
So you need twenty dollars more.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “So you need twenty more dollars.”
You can cheer, or say “So you need twenty more dollars.”

partial.message: Do the math: “So you need twenty more dollars.”

off_topic.message: He has thirty of fifty. Say “So you need twenty more dollars.”

npc.text: Yes. I will save for two more weeks.

examples: Good luck! I am sure you can do it.
Good luck! You can do it.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Good luck! I am sure you can do it.”

routes.meaning: Mia offers to help Sam save.
examples: I will lend you some money.
routes.hint: You can offer help, or say “Good luck! I am sure you can do it.”

partial.message: Cheer for Sam: “Good luck! I am sure you can do it.”

off_topic.message: Sam will save two more weeks. Say “Good luck! I am sure you can do it.”

npc.text: That is so kind! Thank you, Mia.
Thanks, Mia! I will show you my new bike soon.
